message:
chore(deps): migrate asupersync from sibling-path to crates.io v0.3.0 across all fwc crates

Drops the hardcoded `path = "../../../asupersync"` dependency — which
only resolved on the author's workstation (via the /data/projects
layout) — in favor of the freshly published crates.io v0.3.0 release.
Fresh clones, remote RCH workers, and CI now resolve asupersync without
needing a sibling checkout.

v0.3.0 (2026-04-21) ships 2500+ commits since 0.2.9, including the
digest-0.11 / sha1+sha2+hmac dependency wave, three production
concurrency fixes (observability RwLock self-deadlock, DNS coalesce
window, task wakeup ordering), and a large test-compile hygiene pass —
relevant for this workspace's tracing/TLS connector surface.

- connectors/discord/Cargo.toml,
  connectors/slack/Cargo.toml: `path = "../../../asupersync", …,
  features = ["tls", "tls-native-roots"]` → `version = "0.3.0", …`.
- crates/fcp-async-core/Cargo.toml: same swap, preserves the extra
  `tracing-integration` feature used for cross-cutting spans.
- crates/fcp-graphql/Cargo.toml,
  crates/fcp-telemetry/Cargo.toml: `path = …, default-features = false`
  → `version = "0.3.0", default-features = false`.
- crates/fcp-host/Cargo.toml: `path = "../../../asupersync"` →
  `version = "0.3.0"`.
- crates/fcp-tailscale/Cargo.toml: same swap, TLS features preserved.
- Cargo.lock: refreshed via `cargo update` to resolve asupersync 0.3.0
  plus the related transitive bumps (sha1/sha2, signal-hook, subtle
  removed, icu_locid/zerofrom/zerovec family, etc.).
